items.title: Eraser is positioned around technical docs and diagrams, not generic whiteboarding
items.summary: The public product surface emphasizes diagrams, docs, AI diagram generation, and engineering workflows, which points to a technical design collaboration domain.
items.implication: Model the business around recurring engineering design workflows rather than open-ended brainstorming.

items.title: Monetization appears to combine seat-based plans with usage-based API and AI economics
items.summary: Public pricing exposes seat tiers and usage-based API concepts, implying a hybrid SaaS plus metered revenue model.
items.implication: Model balanced scorecards and product deployment around both workspace seats and metered platform workflows.

items.title: Repository and codebase-aware workflows are a meaningful differentiator for technical design tools
items.summary: Eraser documentation and public surfaces highlight GitHub and codebase visualization workflows, which move the product beyond static drawing.
items.implication: Include code-connected bricks, capabilities, and KPIs for documentation drift reduction and repo-linked adoption.

items.title: Enterprise trust features are necessary for broad rollout in larger engineering organizations
items.summary: Security and enterprise documentation indicate admin, identity, and compliance requirements are part of the buying motion.
items.implication: Treat governance, auditability, and controlled sharing as core product capability areas rather than only back-office features.

items.title: Technical design tools win when they lower blank-page cost and speed credible first drafts
items.summary: Engineering productivity research and AI workflow adoption both point to faster artifact creation as a real wedge when quality remains acceptable.
items.implication: Use first-draft speed and acceptance as leading KPIs rather than tracking artifact volume alone.

items.title: Docs-plus-diagrams is stronger than either surface alone for repeat engineering use
items.summary: A markdown-native doc surface paired with diagrams supports review, onboarding, and lasting knowledge better than standalone canvases.
items.implication: Model combined document and figure workflows as a central product experience, not a secondary feature.

items.title: Embeds and cross-tool distribution matter because technical design work happens across many surfaces
items.summary: Engineering knowledge is consumed in wikis, pull requests, docs, and internal tools, so distribution quality affects retention.
items.implication: Include export, embed, and API-driven delivery bricks instead of modeling authoring only.

items.title: Reference libraries and standards programs are a credible expansion path in larger accounts
items.summary: Once teams start using technical diagrams, platform or architecture teams often try to standardize notation, templates, and review patterns.
items.implication: Model shared template and reference-library adoption as a strategic expansion vector.

items.title: Async review quality is a major product value lever for engineering managers
items.summary: The more design review can happen with context in-product, the less teams rely on calendar time and fragmented comments.
items.implication: Track design review completion and async completion rates as first-class success metrics.

items.title: Governed sharing is a buying criterion, not only a risk-control afterthought
items.summary: External sharing of technical artifacts to customers, auditors, and partners means permissioning and export controls directly shape deal viability.
items.implication: Treat safe publishing and link governance as part of the customer-facing product surface.
